summary: Backend-only testing of AES-256-GCM encryption upgrade complete. All 18 tests passed covering: document upload/download/delete with new encryption, message encryption, digital wallet encryption, vault security info endpoint, audit trail, and per-estate key isolation.
passed_tests: POST /api/documents/upload: Encrypts with AES-256-GCM and stores in cloud storage (LocalStorage dev)
GET /api/documents/{doc_id}/download: Correctly decrypts AES-256-GCM and returns plaintext content
GET /api/documents/{doc_id}/preview: Decrypts and returns inline with correct Content-Disposition
DELETE /api/documents/{doc_id}: Removes from both MongoDB and cloud storage (verified 404 after delete)
POST /api/messages: Creates message with encrypted title and content (AES-256-GCM)
POST /api/digital-wallet: Creates wallet entry with encrypted password using per-estate key
GET /api/digital-wallet/{estate_id}: Returns decrypted passwords for estate owner
GET /api/vault/security-info/{estate_id}: Returns encryption metadata (AES-256-GCM, PBKDF2-SHA256, per-estate keys, SOC 2 compliance)
Audit trail: Document upload creates entry in security_audit_log collection
Audit trail: Document download creates audit entry
New estate creation: Automatically generates encryption_salt (32 bytes hex-encoded)
Existing estate: Has encryption_salt (lazily generated for legacy estates)
Per-estate key isolation: Different estates have different encryption salts

encryption_verification.key_scope: Per-estate derived keys from master key + estate-specific salt

critical_code_review_comments: Document upload endpoint uses query parameters (not form data) for estate_id/name/category alongside file upload - working correctly
Per-estate encryption salts are properly generated as 32-byte random values stored as hex
Audit trail entries are being created for document.upload, document.download, document.delete actions
Cloud storage files are stored as base64-encoded AES-256-GCM ciphertext with version header
seed_data_creation: Test data created during tests was cleaned up in TestCleanup class
context_for_next_testing_agent: AES-256-GCM encryption upgrade testing complete. All major features verified: document encryption/storage, message encryption, wallet encryption, audit trail, vault security info endpoint, per-estate key isolation. Cloud storage uses LocalStorage at /app/backend/vault_storage/ in dev environment. MongoDB security_audit_log collection has 41+ entries. Rate limiting on dev-login endpoint - use session-scoped fixtures in conftest.py to avoid 429 errors.

mocked_apis.S3 storage: Uses LocalStorage (filesystem) in dev/preview environment - this is by design, not a mock
